evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis layout cleanly shows log-scale loss decreasing and cosine LR decaying; color-coupled axis ticks satisfy the brief's hint.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (not Type 42) DejaVu Sans — the Matplotlib default — so text is not selectable/editable.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for loss and red (#d62728) for LR are colorblind-distinguishable; no directional green/red confusion since both series are directional but clearly labeled.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() applied on a 7×4 figure; no gaping whitespace, no label overlaps, log y-range spans full loss dynamic range, LR y-axis starts at 0.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend present with both series, axis tick colors match their lines per brief, caption accurately describes what is shown; no obvious Reviewer-2 issues.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype not set to 42; pdffonts reports BMQQDV+DejaVuSans as Type 3 (not TrueType/Type 42), meaning text is not selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (15 KB), figure.png (70 KB), figure.svg (40 KB), caption.tex (371 B), gen_figure.py present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical findings (loss ≈2.19→0.21, LR 10⁻³→0), names both axes/scales, mentions dashed style and color-coded ticks.

overall_strengths: Clean dual-axis design with color-coupled axis ticks/labels that directly implements the brief's guidance.
Caption is specific and quantitative, citing numerical values for both loss and learning rate ranges.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) not set, so the PDF contains Type 3 bitmapped fonts rather than embedded TrueType — text is not selectable or editable.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; no explicit font choice to match a publication style, and font size hierarchy is minimal (all labels at 11 pt, legend at 9 pt).
